summary: Completed backend-only regression validation for Android native-output polling and fallback UI queue messaging. Added and executed static tests for JNI poll path, MainActivity bridge polling, index.html stepToUI dispatch loop, fallback packed step message emissions, and expanded waveform options in view.js.
action_items: Run on-device Android runtime verification to confirm step lights advance visually during active playback, since this iteration is static/template-level backend validation only.
Optionally add instrumentation logs around pollOutputEvents frequency/latency under load to catch dropped UI events.
critical_code_review_comments: Requested native→JS poll chain is structurally complete: JNI nativePollOutputEvents -> AndroidHost.pollOutputEvents -> index pollNativeEvents -> stepToUI listener dispatch.
Fallback queue logic structurally covers generate/clearPattern/requestPatternDump/setStepPacked and playback movement via packed kind=1 and kind=2 payloads.
Waveform selector now includes additional options (Triangle, Sine) beyond Saw and Square.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Added 4 regression tests focused on pollOutputEvents wiring, fallback packed step emissions, queue updates for pattern operations, and waveform option coverage. All tests pass locally via pytest.
rca of the issue: No active failures found in this scope. Prior risk was missing/partial propagation of fallback events into JS; current code demonstrates complete polling-based propagation path and packed event handling at template level. No MOCKED APIs used.
